name: ntfy Push Notification API
what_it_does: Open-source pub/sub HTTP push notification service — send push notifications to phones and desktops from any script or CI pipeline via simple HTTP PUT/POST, with self-hosted and cloud-hosted options.
use_cases: Sending push notifications to developer phones from scripts, CI pipelines, and agents
Real-time alerting from monitoring agents without requiring app store approval
Pub/sub message delivery between automation agents via topic-based subscriptions
Server health and job completion notifications without paid notification service
Building lightweight alerting systems with self-hosted ntfy server for privacy
not_for: Production customer-facing push notifications (use FCM/APNs via OneSignal or Firebase)
Rich media notifications with images and action buttons (use OneSignal)
SMS or email delivery — ntfy is push/websocket only
best_when: A developer or agent needs simple, free push notifications from scripts or automation without complex SDK setup — especially for self-hosted or privacy-conscious deployments.
avoid_when: You need customer-facing push notifications with analytics, segmentation, or app store distribution.

auth.notes: Optional authentication — public topics require no auth. API key or HTTP Basic auth for protected topics on self-hosted or ntfy.sh Pro. Topic-based access control. Anonymous publishing to public topics.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: public topics on ntfy.sh, 250 messages/day on public server
pricing.paid_tiers: ntfy Pro: $3/month for reserved topics, email support, higher limits
pricing.notes: Completely free to self-host (Go binary, Docker image available). Public server ntfy.sh free for public topics. Pro plan for reserved/private topics. Remarkable simplicity — curl | ntfy with no setup.

performance.rate_limits: 250 messages/day on public server; unlimited on self-hosted

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Excellent documentation with curl examples for every feature. Minimal API — agents can send notifications with a single HTTP request. WebSocket and SSE for receiving messages. JSON or plain text payloads.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All publishes are fire-and-forget — no state. Message deduplication via message ID header. No transaction semantics needed.
known_agent_gotchas: Public topics on ntfy.sh are world-readable — use private topics for sensitive alerts
Message history limited to 24 hours on public server — messages expire
No delivery confirmation without subscriber polling — fire-and-forget semantics
Rate limits on public server (250/day) — self-host for agent use cases with higher volume

security_score_components.security_notes: Public topics have no security — anyone can publish/subscribe. Protected topics require auth. Self-hosted allows full security control. TLS enforced on ntfy.sh cloud. Open-source for audit. Topic names are effectively passwords for public topics.
